title.rendered: AMI at Embedded World 2019 in Nuremberg, Germany: February 26 – 28, 2019

content.rendered:
NORCROSS, GEORGIA: – AMI, a global leader in BIOS and UEFI firmware, server and remote management tools, data storage products and unique solutions based on the Linux® and Android™ operating systems, is pleased to announce its participation at Embedded World 2019 in Nuremberg, Germany from February 26 to 28, 2019.

The Embedded World Exhibition & Conference is an international embedded technology trade show that takes place every year in Nuremberg, Germany. Every year Embedded World attracts more than 1,000 exhibitors and 30,000 attendees from around the globe, where exhibitors showcase the latest developments in embedded technology. Attendees have the opportunity to learn about these new technologies, while also networking with representatives from different technology companies.

AMI will once again exhibit at Embedded World in Hall 2, Booth #2-200 at the Nuremberg Exhibition Centre. At this year’s show, visitors to the AMI booth will have the opportunity to speak with AMI experts about the company’s latest technologies. AMI will present numerous firmware offerings, covering technologies such as Embedded Controllers (EC) and Baseboard Management Controller (BMC) firmware solutions.

Visitors can also learn more about the latest enhancements to AMI’s flagship Aptio® V UEFI BIOS Firmware that enable OEM/ODM partners to deliver trusted, unique and innovative user experiences to their end customers. Recent Aptio V innovations on display at Embedded World will include remote management via the Redfish® schema, boot time reduction with AMI’s Performance Measurement Tool (PMT), UEFI firmware hardware debugging with AMI’s Trace Hub Debugger, porting/testing and methodologies for firmware security and more.

To learn more about Aptio V UEFI Firmware from AMI, please visit ami.com/products/aptio-v/.

Redfish® is a registered trademark of the Distributed Management Task Force. All other trademarks and registered trademarks are the property of their respective owners.
